info.title: Plunk API
info.description: Open-source email platform API for transactional emails, campaigns, and marketing automation

ApiKeyAuth.description: API Key authentication. **Secret keys (sk_*)** are required for all endpoints except `/v1/track`. **Public keys (pk_*)** only work with the `/v1/track` endpoint for client-side event tracking. The project is automatically derived from the key.

post.description:
Send a transactional email via the public API. Automatically creates or updates the recipient contact.

**Required content:** either a `template` ID, **or** both `subject` and `body`. Template fields can be overridden by explicit request fields.

**Sender:** `from` is required unless using a template that already has a `from` configured. The sender's domain must be verified.

**Multiple recipients:** when `to` is an array, each recipient is processed sequentially with its own contact upsert and rendered email — there is no batch-send semantics. Sending is always immediate; for scheduled sends, use a Campaign.

**Attachments:** up to 10 attachments per email and 10 MB total by default. The total message size cannot exceed 40 MB.

to.description: Recipient email(s). Can be a string, an object with {name, email}, or an array of either.

subject.description: Email subject. Required if no `template` is provided. Cannot contain newline characters.

body.description: Email body (HTML). Required if no `template` is provided.

template.description: Template ID to use for this email. When provided, uses the template's subject, body, from, and reply-to settings. You can override these by explicitly providing subject, body, from, or reply fields in the request. Template variables are populated from the data field.

from.description: Sender email address (requires verified domain). Required unless using a template that has a 'from' address configured. Can be a string (e.g., 'hello@example.com') or an object with {name, email} (e.g., {name: 'My App', email: 'hello@example.com'}).

name.description: **Deprecated.** Sender display name. Prefer `from: { name, email }`. Used only as a fallback when `from` is a string and no name is set there.

subscribed.description: Subscription state to apply to the recipient. For **new** contacts, defaults to `false` on `/v1/send`. For **existing** contacts, omitting this preserves their current state — pass `true` or `false` to explicitly change it. A change emits `contact.subscribed` or `contact.unsubscribed`.

data.description:
Variables for template rendering and contact data updates. Each value can be:
- A primitive (string, number, boolean) — saved on the contact and available as a template variable.
- `null` — deletes the field from the contact.
- An empty string — skipped (does not overwrite existing data).
- An object `{ value, persistent: false }` — used for this send only, not stored on the contact (good for one-shot password reset codes, magic links).

Reserved keys (`id`, `plunk_id`, `plunk_email`, `email`, `unsubscribeUrl`, `subscribeUrl`, `manageUrl`) are silently filtered out.

headers.description: Custom email headers. Header names cannot contain `\r\n`. Header values are limited to 998 characters and cannot contain `\r\n` (header injection is rejected).

attachments.description: Email attachments. Default cap: 10 attachments and 10 MB total. The full message size cannot exceed 40 MB.

filename.description: Attachment filename. Cannot contain newline or quote characters.

contentId.description: Content-ID for inline images. Required when `disposition` is `inline`. Reference the image in the email body via ``.

disposition.description: Use `inline` together with `contentId` to embed images in the body. Use `attachment` (the default) for downloadable files.

value.subject: Password Reset Request
value.body:
Reset Your Password

Click the link to reset: {{resetLink}}

withNames.summary: Email with recipient and sender names

withTemplate.description: Send email using a template. Provide the template ID and any data for template variables. The template's subject, body, from address, and reply-to will be used automatically.

withTemplateOverride.description: You can override template values by providing subject, body, from, or reply fields. This example overrides the template's subject line.

email.description: Plunk email record ID. Use this to correlate webhook events (which include this ID as 'emailId' in the event data) with your send requests.

post.description:
Track an event for a contact. Automatically creates or upserts the contact, then records the event. Tracked events can be used as workflow triggers, segment filters, and audience filters.

**Reserved event names** (rejected with `VALIDATION_ERROR` and code `reserved_event`): anything matching `email.*`, `contact.subscribed`, `contact.unsubscribed`, `segment.<slug>.entry`, `segment.<slug>.exit`. These are emitted by Plunk itself.

**No idempotency**: re-tracking the same event creates a new event record.

email.description: Contact email. The contact is auto-created if it doesn't exist.

event.description: Event name. Cannot match the reserved patterns above.

subscribed.description: Subscription state to apply to the contact. **New** contacts default to subscribed (`true`). **Existing** contacts keep their current state unless you pass an explicit value here. Pass `false` to track an event without resubscribing an unsubscribed contact.

data.description: Contact data and one-off event variables. Persistent values (primitives, plain objects) are saved on the contact and become available as template variables. Pass `{ value, persistent: false }` for one-shot variables that should not be stored on the contact (e.g. order IDs, transaction details). `null` deletes a field. Empty strings are ignored. Reserved keys are filtered out — see the contacts concept page.

post.description: Verify an email address for validity, check if it's from a disposable domain or personal email provider, verify MX records, and detect potential typos with suggestions.

valid.description: Whether the email appears to be valid overall

isDisposable.description: Whether the email is from a disposable/temporary email domain

isAlias.description: Whether the email is from a forwarding/alias service

isTypo.description: Whether a potential typo was detected in the email address

isPlusAddressed.description: Whether the email uses plus addressing (contains a + in the local part)

isPersonalEmail.description: Whether the email is from a personal/free email provider (Gmail, Hotmail, Yahoo, etc.)

domainExists.description: Whether the domain exists in DNS (has NS records)

hasWebsite.description: Whether the domain has a website (has DNS A or AAAA records) - informational only

hasMxRecords.description: Whether the domain has MX records configured for email delivery

suggestedEmail.description: Suggested correction if a typo was detected (optional)

reasons.description: Array of human-readable reasons describing the verification results

reasons: Possible typo detected, did you mean gmail.com?
Domain does not exist (no nameservers found)

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid or missing API key

get.description: Get a paginated list of contacts with cursor-based pagination.

cursor.description: Cursor for the next page. Pass this back as the `cursor` query parameter to fetch the next page.

total.description: Total count. Only populated on the first page (when no `cursor` is supplied); subsequent pages return `0` to avoid the recount cost.

post.description: Create a new contact or update existing (upsert by email)

get.description: Get a single contact by ID

patch.description: Update an existing contact's email, subscription state, or `data` fields.

email.description: Change the contact's email address. Returns 409 if another contact in the project already uses this email.

subscribed.description: Update subscription state. Flipping this fires `contact.subscribed` or `contact.unsubscribed`.

data.description: Patch contact data. `null` deletes a key, empty strings are ignored, primitives are stored. Reserved keys are silently filtered out.

409.description: An existing contact already uses the email you're trying to set.

get.description: Get a paginated list of email templates
Get a paginated list of email campaigns

from.description: Sender email address (must be from verified domain)

segmentId.description: Segment ID (required if audienceType is SEGMENT)

audienceFilter.description: Filter conditions (required if audienceType is FILTERED)

post.description: Send a campaign immediately or schedule for future delivery

scheduledFor.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when to send the campaign. Omit or set to null for immediate send.

tags.name: Public API
tags.description: Public API endpoints for sending emails and tracking events
